summary: Unfocused but accepted ground-stage decomp cleanup and matching PR. The main reusable change was factoring the repeated stage-gobj callback setup sequence into Ground_SetupStageCallbacks in gr/inlines.h, then replacing dozens of nearly identical per-stage implementations. The PR also matched or nearly matched several grpushon functions, added StageCallbacks/StageData/data tables for Push On and other stages, typed Push On ground vars and light-object helpers, and added a baselib HSD_LObjSetNext inline used by lb_00F9 and mnStageSel. decomp.dev reported +1740 matched bytes, 8 new grpushon function matches, and 14 unmatched-item improvements, with one small grpura regression.
what_changed: Added static inline Ground_SetupStageCallbacks(HSD_GObj*, StageCallbacks*) in src/melee/gr/inlines.h. It clears Ground x8_callback and xC_callback, installs GX link grDisplay_801C5DB0 with priority/link args 3 and 0, copies callback3 to x1C_callback when present, calls callback0 when present, and installs callback2 as an HSD_GObj proc with priority 4.

Replaced the repeated callback setup boilerplate across many ground files with Ground_SetupStageCallbacks(gobj, callbacks), including figure stages, target-test stages, Garden, Fourside, Shrine, Story, Ice Mountain, Pura, and others.

Implemented or improved stage object creation/setup functions using the shared helper, including grCorneria_801DD534, grHomeRun_8021C82C, grOldKongo_8020F52C, grIceMt_801F71E8, grPura_80211E08, and grPushOn_802183E4.

Added StageCallbacks data arrays for multiple stages shown in the diff, including grCn_803E1D8C, grHr_803E8140, grOk_803E658C, grOp_803E6688, and grPushOn_803E7AC8, with flag fields such as 0x80000000, 0x40000000, 0x20000000, and 0xC0000000 preserved in table entries.

Added StageData grPushOn_803E7B10 for PUSHON, pointing at grPushOn_803E7AC8, /GrNPo.dat, stage lifecycle callbacks, dynamics callback grPushOn_80219458, and collision/query callback grPushOn_80219528.

Substantially decompiled grpushon.c: grPushOn_802183E4, grPushOn_802184CC, grPushOn_802185A0, grPushOn_802186C8, grPushOn_802187A8, grPushOn_80218ED4, grPushOn_80218FC0, and grPushOn_80219458 are present in the excerpt as C instead of placeholders.
Added struct grPushOn_GroundVars in gr/types.h with gobj, lobjs[20], lobj_flags[20], count, point_light, and spot_light fields, then inserted it into Ground's stage-specific union as pushon.
Updated grpushon.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or imprecise signatures to typed signatures for grPushOn_802183E4, grPushOn_80218ED4, grPushOn_80218FC0, fn_802190A0, and grPushOn_802190D0.
Added HSD_LObjSetNext inline to baselib/lobj.h using HSD_ASSERT(0x136, lobj) before assigning lobj->next.
Replaced direct lobj->next writes plus duplicated assert patterns with HSD_LObjSetNext in lb_80011AC4 and mnStageSel_8025A998_OnEnter.

Implemented grHomeRun_8021C754 setup logic, including Ground_801C49F8 storage, stage_info.unk8C flags, spawning objects 1 and 0xA through grHomeRun_8021C82C, camera/blast-zone calculations using Stage_UnkSetVec3TCam_Offset, Stage_GetCamBoundsRightOffset, and Stage_GetBlastZoneRightOffset.

Standardized several OSReport callsites and in many files switched string-literal filenames to __FILE__; evidence is visible in grfigure2.c, grfigure3.c, grgarden.c, grshrine.c, grtseak.c, grtzelda.c, and others.
smart_moves: Factoring the common Ground_GetStageGObj success path into Ground_SetupStageCallbacks reduced repeated C in dozens of near-identical stage setup functions while preserving the original callback order, null checks, GX link setup, and proc priority.

Keeping Ground_SetupStageCallbacks as a static inline in gr/inlines.h is a decomp-friendly tactic: it allows repeated source simplification without necessarily forcing a shared out-of-line symbol that would disturb matching.
The helper uses GET_GROUND(gobj), grDisplay_801C5DB0, GObj_SetupGXLink, and HSD_GObj_SetupProc exactly as the repeated stage code did, which made it usable across many stage files with small diffs.
The PR converted large StageCallbacks tables from implicit data into named arrays near their stage source, improving .data matching and making gobj_id indexing explicit.
Adding HSD_LObjSetNext captured the library idiom, including the original lobj assert line number 0x136, and removed hand-written next-pointer mutations from callers.
Typing grPushOn_GroundVars made the newly matched Push On functions much clearer, especially light-object preservation, lobj flags, count, point_light, and spot_light handling.
The Push On light setup functions reuse the new HSD_LObjSetNext helper when appending loaded point/spot light descriptors, avoiding duplicated assert/assignment code.
The author explicitly identified the helper extraction as the main part of the PR, making the broad mechanical cleanup reviewable despite many touched files.
decomp_lessons: When many stage files contain byte-identical callback setup boilerplate, first isolate the exact semantic sequence into a static inline helper, then substitute it broadly; this can improve readability while preserving match-sensitive codegen.

Stage setup wrappers generally follow the pattern: callbacks = &stage_callbacks[gobj_id], gobj = Ground_GetStageGObj(gobj_id), if non-null setup callbacks, else OSReport("%s:%d: couldn t get gobj(id=%d)\n", file, line, gobj_id), return gobj.
Ground_SetupStageCallbacks preserves a specific order that future decomp should avoid reordering: clear x8/xC, setup GX link, install callback3 to x1C, invoke callback0, install callback2 proc.
Use StageCallbacks arrays to model stage object behavior tables; the fifth field carries important flags and should be preserved as observed constants rather than guessed away.
Small baselib accessors can be critical for matching because they preserve asserts and line numbers while hiding direct struct mutation.
Replacing direct field writes with an inline helper is safe only when the helper reproduces asserts and side effects; HSD_LObjSetNext asserts lobj before assigning next.
Promoting UNKNOWN prototypes to typed prototypes in headers helps downstream files match and documents function roles, but the evidence here is limited to grpushon.h and grhomerun.h changes.
Ground union members should be named for the stage-specific vars when enough field evidence exists; grPushOn_GroundVars demonstrates using offset comments and known array sizes to constrain layout.
Broad cleanup PRs can be acceptable in this repo when they consolidate a common pattern; reviewer ribbanya explicitly said the scope was preferred over splitting into multiple smaller PRs.
naming_conventions: Shared helper introduced as Ground_SetupStageCallbacks, following the Ground_* prefix for ground-system utilities.
Stage callback arrays use short stage prefixes plus address-like suffixes, e.g. grCn_803E1D8C, grHr_803E8140, grOk_803E658C, grOp_803E6688, grPushOn_803E7AC8.
Stage-specific variable structs follow gr<Stage>_GroundVars naming, e.g. grPushOn_GroundVars.
Ground union member for Push On is named pushon, matching the stage name rather than an address.
Light-object helper follows baselib style HSD_LObjSetNext paired with existing HSD_LObjGetNext.
Unidentified game functions still retain address-based names such as grPushOn_80218ED4 and grHomeRun_8021C82C, with prototypes improved where signatures were known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used a static inline rather than an external function for common stage callback setup to avoid adding a new call boundary where original code likely had repeated inline code.
Preserved match-sensitive null checks around callbacks->callback3, callback0, and callback2.
Preserved GObj_SetupGXLink(gobj, grDisplay_801C5DB0, 3, 0) and HSD_GObj_SetupProc(gobj, callbacks->callback2, 4) constants in the shared helper.
Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on around grCorneria_801DD534 in the excerpt, suggesting an explicit codegen control tactic for that function.

Used PAD_STACK markers in several grpushon functions, such as grPushOn_802185A0, grPushOn_802186C8, grPushOn_802187A8, grPushOn_80218ED4, and grPushOn_80218FC0, indicating stack-shape padding for matching.
Materialized .data callback arrays to improve data-section matching; decomp.dev reported .data gains for grcorneria, grhomerun, groldkongo, and grpushon.
Used exact OSReport strings and source line constants, sometimes replacing filenames with __FILE__, but still preserving the observed message text "couldn t get gobj(id=%d)".
Preserved HSD_ASSERT line number 0x136 in HSD_LObjSetNext to match previous direct assert behavior.

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.text: The author described the PR as unfocused and said most of it factored a very common pattern into a helper function.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: The reviewer approved and said the scope was not a problem; in fact, it was preferred over multiple smaller PRs.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Reported matched code 58.52% (+0.04%, +1740 bytes), 8 new matches, 14 improvements in unmatched items, and 1 unmatched-item regression in grPura_802125F0 (-48 bytes, 75.26% to 72.51%).

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further use Ground_SetupStageCallbacks in remaining gr files.
Check whether the grPura_802125F0 regression from this PR was later recovered.
Search for direct lobj->next assignments that could be converted to HSD_LObjSetNext.
Search for remaining StageCallbacks setup boilerplate not yet using Ground_SetupStageCallbacks.
Investigate whether grPushOn_802190D0 and fn_802192A4 were matched in later Push On work.
Compare later changes to grPushOn_GroundVars field names and offsets for improved naming.
